diagrams.title: Singular vs Non-Singular Transformation
diagrams.description: Comparison showing how singular transformation collapses dimensions while non-singular preserves them
diagrams.purpose: Understand geometric difference between singular and non-singular transformations

diagrams.title: Kernel and Range Visualization
diagrams.description: Domain, codomain showing kernel (pre-image of zero) and range (image of transformation)
elements: T arrows from kernel to zero
T arrows from domain to range
diagrams.purpose: Visualize kernel (null space) and range (image) as subspaces

diagrams.title: Rank-Nullity Theorem
diagrams.description: Visual representation of Rank-Nullity: domain splits into kernel and complementary space that maps isomorphically to range
elements: Range W (smaller than V for singular)
diagrams.purpose: Understand decomposition of domain under linear transformation

diagrams.title: One-to-One and Onto Mappings
diagrams.description: Commutative diagram showing injective (one-to-one) and surjective (onto) properties
elements: One-to-one: each output has at most one input arrow
Onto: each element in W has at least one preimage
diagrams.purpose: Distinguish between one-to-one, onto, and bijective transformations
